steps.prompt: Create a list of 5 US states with capitals in columns A and B starting at row 3.
Add a SUM formula in B8 that totals B2:B7, and an AVERAGE in B9.
Sort the selected data by Amount descending. Keep the header row in place.
Create a new sheet named Revenue.
Rename sheet Revenue to Q1 Revenue.
Clear column C (the Notes column).
Create an expense table with columns: Date, Description, Amount in A3:C3 with 3 sample rows.
Now add a Tax column in D with 10% of Amount.
Add a total row summing Amount and Tax columns.
Add a Bonus column that is 15% of Salary.

Create a monthly budget strictly within A2:E7: headers in A2:E2 = Category, Jan, Feb, Mar, Total; 4 expense categories in A3:A6; sample dollar amounts in B3:D6; SUM formulas in E3:E6 and B7:E7 for totals. Do not write outside A2:E7. No titles or extra text. Use set_values for headers and numbers; use set_formula only for E3:E6 and B7:E7.

Fill A2 with the count of data rows (excluding the header) in the Sales sheet, and A3 with the count of data rows (excluding the header) in the Inventory sheet. Write plain numbers only (set_values), no formulas, no titles, and do not edit other cells.

Fill in the morphological forms for the 5 Hebrew roots in column A (rows 2-6). Row 1 contains the headers describing what each column needs. For each root, provide: B = transliteration of the root consonants, C = Qal perfect 3rd person masculine singular verb form, D = Qal imperfect 3rd person masculine singular verb form, E = common noun (singular masculine if applicable), F = noun (singular feminine if applicable, otherwise leave blank), G = adjective masculine form (if one exists from this root, otherwise leave blank), H = adjective feminine form (if applicable, otherwise leave blank), I = semantic domain (one or two words, e.g. 'writing', 'learning'). Write Hebrew text in Hebrew script for columns C-H. Use set_values only. Do not add titles, formulas, or extra rows.

Now add 3 more Hebrew roots and their forms. Add the roots פקד (paqad), שׁפט (shapat), and ברך (barakh) in A7:A9, then fill B7:I9 with their morphological forms following the same pattern as the existing rows. Use set_values only.
Within A2:D4, create 3 sample rows with Item, Qty, Price, Total. Use set_values for Item/Qty/Price and set_formula for Total in each row as =B(row)*C(row). Do not write outside A2:D4. No titles.
Append 2 more rows in A5:D6 following the same pattern. Do not modify A2:D4. Use set_values for Item/Qty/Price and set_formula for Total in each new row.
For each existing input in A2:A4, fill outputs in B2:D4 aligned to the headers. Use set_values only. Do not write to A.
Add two new inputs delta and epsilon in A5:A6, then fill B5:D6 with outputs aligned to the headers. Use set_values only. Do not modify existing rows.
Fill A2:B4 with three pairs of numbers (any small integers). Leave C empty.
In C2:C4 write =A2+B2, =A3+B3, =A4+B4 respectively. Use set_values only.
Write a list of 5 of your favorite AI engineers in column A starting at A2.
Add a column with their special ability next to their names. Include a header 'Special Ability'.
Explain what this sheet contains in 1-2 sentences. Answer only; do not propose any spreadsheet changes.
Look at the selection and produce observations only (uniques, column profile, sample rows). Do not make any changes.
Copy A1:B3 to D1:E3. Adjust formulas and references appropriately.
Move A1:B3 to F1:G3. Adjust formulas and references appropriately.
Set the header row A1:D1 to bold, center alignment, and a light gray background (#EEEEEE).
Format D2:D4 with number format 0.00.
Insert one new column at column B and set B1 to 'Inserted'. Do not alter other data.
Delete column C.
Delete the sheet named Temp.
Set data validation on B2:B10 to a list with allowed values Low, Medium, High. Empty allowed.
Apply conditional formatting to A2:A5: highlight cells with values greater than 12 with bold text and a yellow background (#FFFF99).

Normalize the City names in the selection: trim whitespace and convert to Title Case (e.g., 'lincroft' -> 'Lincroft', 'RIDGEWOOD' -> 'Ridgewood', 'CHERRY HILL' -> 'Cherry Hill'). Do not change any other columns. Use set_values only.

Normalize the City names in the selection: trim whitespace and convert to Title Case (e.g., 'lincroft' -> 'Lincroft', 'RIDGEWOOD' -> 'Ridgewood', 'CHERRY HILL' -> 'Cherry Hill'). Do not change any other columns. Use set_values only.
Normalize the City names in the selection: trim whitespace and convert to Title Case. Use set_values only. Do not add titles.
Normalize the City names in the selection: trim whitespace and convert to Title Case. Use set_values only. Do not add titles.
Normalize the City names in the selection: trim whitespace and convert to Title Case. You may use transform_range with op=normalize_city. Make changes only within the selection.
Observe only: return uniques, profile, and a few sample rows. Do not propose or apply any write commands.
Within the selection only, normalize values by trimming whitespace and converting to Title Case. Use set_values only. Do not touch cells outside the selection.
Clean up the Name column: trim whitespace, fix inconsistent casing to proper Title Case (e.g. 'JOHN SMITH' -> 'John Smith', 'jane doe' -> 'Jane Doe'). Only modify column A. Use set_values only.
Now lowercase all email addresses in column C. Only modify column C. Use set_values only.

Add three new columns: F1='Annual Total' with SUM of Q1-Q4 for each product, G1='Best Quarter' showing the MAX quarterly value, H1='Growth %' showing the percentage change from Q1 to Q4 as a formula like =(E2-B2)/B2. Use set_values for headers and set_formula for the computed cells. Write headers in row 1 and formulas in rows 2-5.
Look at this data and tell me what problems you see.
Fix the status column: normalize all values to lowercase ('active', 'inactive', 'pending'). Fix the typo 'actve' to 'active'. Only modify column C. Use set_values only.
Analyze this revenue data. Which month had the worst performance and why might that be? What is the overall trend?

Add a Profit column in E (E1='Profit') with formulas E2:E13 computing Revenue minus Expenses for each month. Then add a summary row: A14='Summary', B14=SUM of all revenue, C14=SUM of all expenses, E14=SUM of all profit. Use set_values for labels and set_formula for computed cells.

Create an inventory table with headers: Item, SKU, Quantity, Unit Price, Total Value. Place headers in A1:E1. Add 4 sample items (use realistic office supply items). Use set_values only for everything including the Total Value column (compute the values yourself, don't use formulas).

Add a Reorder Level column in F with header 'Reorder Level'. Set reorder levels based on the items: items with quantity below 20 should have reorder level of 50, others should have reorder level of 25. Use set_values only.
Add 3 more items in rows 6-8 following the same pattern. Include the Reorder Level column too. Make sure the SKU format matches the existing pattern. Use set_values only. Do not modify existing rows.

Now add a summary section below the data. In A10 put 'Total Items:', B10 should have a COUNT formula for column A data rows. In A11 put 'Total Value:', B11 should have a SUM formula for column E data rows. In A12 put 'Avg Price:', B12 should have an AVERAGE formula for column D data rows. Use set_values for labels and set_formula for the computed cells.

Add two new budget categories: 'Entertainment' with budgeted=150 and actual=200, and 'Savings' with budgeted=500 and actual=500. Insert them as rows 7-8, pushing the current Totals row down to row 9. Update the Totals formulas in row 9 to include the new rows. Use insert_rows, set_values for the new data, and set_formula for the updated totals.
Observe the data in this sheet. Describe what you see including any isolated values.

Create a proper summary table in A1:C4 with headers Region, Value, Pct of Total. Fill in the three regions (North=100, South=200, East=150) with their values from column C, and calculate each region's percentage of the total (450) as a plain number rounded to 1 decimal. Use set_values only. Do NOT modify F30.

Fill in the Departments summary table using data from the Employees sheet. For each department: B column = number of employees in that department, C column = average salary (rounded to nearest whole number), D column = total salary budget. Compute the values yourself from the Employees sheet data and use set_values only. Engineering has 3 employees (Alice 95k, Carol 98k, Eve 102k). Marketing has 2 (Bob 72k, Frank 75k). Sales has 2 (Dave 68k, Grace 71k).
